# AI Agent Framework Comparison: LangGraph vs CrewAI vs AutoGen

Based on my research, here's a comprehensive comparison of these three leading AI agent frameworks:

## Comparison Table

| Feature | **LangGraph** | **CrewAI** | **AutoGen** |
|---------|---------------|------------|-------------|
| **GitHub Stars** | ~12,000+ | ~20,000+ | ~35,000+ |
| **Developer** | LangChain | CrewAI Inc | Microsoft Research |
| **Architecture** | Graph-based state machine | Role-based multi-agent | Multi-agent conversation |
| **Core Concept** | Nodes and edges with state management | Agents with specific roles and tasks | Autonomous agents with conversation |
| **Language Support** | Python, JavaScript | Python | Python, .NET |
| **Learning Curve** | Medium-High | Low-Medium | Medium |
| **Integration** | LangChain ecosystem | Standalone | Microsoft ecosystem |
| **Pricing** | Open Source + LangSmith (Free tier + Paid plans) | Open Source + Enterprise ($0-199+/mo) | Open Source (Free) |
| **Best For** | Complex workflows, state management | Simple role-based teams, quick prototyping | Research, multi-agent conversations |
| **Strengths** | • Fine-grained control<br>• State persistence<br>• Cycles & branching<br>• Production-ready | • Easy to learn<br>• Intuitive role design<br>• Fast setup<br>• Good documentation | • Research-grade<br>• Microsoft backing<br>• Human-in-the-loop<br>• Code execution |
| **Weaknesses** | • Steeper learning curve<br>• More verbose code<br>• LangChain dependency | • Less flexible for complex flows<br>• Limited state management<br>• Newer ecosystem | • Complex setup<br>• Overhead for simple tasks<br>• Rapid API changes |
| **Use Cases** | • Complex business logic<br>• Stateful applications<br>• Production systems<br>• Multi-step workflows | • Content generation teams<br>• Research teams<br>• Simple automation<br>• MVPs | • Research projects<br>• Code generation<br>• Complex simulations<br>• Enterprise with Microsoft stack |
